name: kava
mechanism: Kavalactones appear to modulate GABAergic and other neurochemical pathways, but pathway-level certainty does not equal universal clinical efficacy.
effects: Systematic review evidence indicates possible short-term anxiety reduction in selected formulations.
contraindications: Avoid in known liver disease or substantial alcohol use.
safetyNotes: Liver safety concerns remain a core caution point, especially with nontraditional extracts and co-exposures.
description: Kava (Piper methysticum) is a Pacific traditional beverage plant studied in standardized extracts for anxiety-related outcomes.
dosage: Use product-specific clinical labeling or trial-standardized extract context; avoid unsupervised high-dose use.

interactions: Potential additive sedation with alcohol, benzodiazepines, and other CNS depressants.
preparation: Traditionally prepared as aqueous root beverage; commercial products include capsules and tincture-like extracts.
region: Traditional use is centered in Oceania/Pacific Island cultures.
traditionalUse: Traditional use in Pacific ceremonial and social contexts is well documented and culturally specific.
summary: Kava has human trial synthesis suggesting anxiolytic potential in some extracts, but hepatotoxicity concerns and product variability require careful risk communication.
